results.title: Softr Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Nick

Image 9: GiveGreat.Gifts GiveGreat.Gifts

•1 review

I liked softr until it deleted my app without warning me. It'd been dormant for a while but was hoping to revisit it and found out they deleted it without giving me a heads up - if you want to hack iteratively I'd lean toward a solution that won't delete your work. Customer service brushed it off saying I'd need to recreate it. That was a good amount of work...

Helpful (2)

Share

Report

41 views 3yr ago

Image 10: Saurao Dalvi

Saurao Dalvi

Image 11: Stare  Stare

View all founder reviews

Other Reviews (39)

Image 12: Yuriy

Yuriy

•1 review

#### What's great

We decided to build an MVP on this platform. When we registered, we got a 7-day trial on the Professional plan. During those 7 days we only tested basic features, templates, etc. We started building our actual MVP after the trial ended—on the Free plan—in a new app. Anything not available on Free was clearly flagged and inactive. [...] #### What's great

user-friendly (14)excellent customer support (8)no-code platform (23)integration with Google Sheets (7)integration with Airtable (10)

results.title: Warbl: Boost engagement and grow your audience effortlessly on X | Product Hunt
results.content:
That said, there's an alternative I've been thinking about: during onboarding, we could present you with different tweet scenarios and ask you to reply in your own words. That way, the tool could analyze your real tone of voice and syntax from scratch.

Definitely something worth exploring. Thanks for the feedback, this is gold! 🙏 [...] Thanks for the feedback, this is exactly the kind of thinking that helps refine the approach!

Nabeel

It is a great product as it is, but I would actually feel a lot comfortable using the tool if I knew the tool has scrapped all my replies in the past, and is using them as a reference to generate replies in my style and syntax!

Alex
Bulkmark

Bulkmark

@nabeeltak Love this idea and totally get where you're coming from! Having the tool learn from your actual past replies would be the dream.

Unfortunately, I don't use the X API directly, which makes scraping your reply history impossible for now. [...] For capturing the user's authentic voice, it's all about layering: the voice profile settings define how you sound, while the AI analyzes the tweet content to understand what you're responding to. Keeping those two separate is key to avoiding generic-sounding replies.

Still refining it, but the results have been pretty solid so far!

Victor Villalobos

Hello,  
  
I like it, but you have an issue with your pro upgrade URL; it gives me a 500 error.

Alex
Bulkmark

Bulkmark

@viktorvillalobos Hey Victor ! Thank's for the feedback.   
  
Is it still not working ? Can you DM me on X @indie\_alx ?

Leo

aha, sign in google failed. Are you sure the product ready?

Alex
Bulkmark

Bulkmark

Felice Della Gatta

I love this! No more staring at your design, squinting while tilting your head left and right like it could magically fix it. Just drop a screenshot in Gleam and iterate. I'm a fan!

Dane Lyons
FollowUp by AssetMule

FollowUp by AssetMule

@its\_felice Thanks!  
  
I'm not opposed to doing a little squinting while internally debating about what's next and why. Investing mental energy in your design is where the real magic happens. I hope Gleam just helps make that process a little easier.

Felice Della Gatta

@duilen of course. I got a little too excited there. 😁
